title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Stakeholder JavaScript Mission
summary: A small public-service organization. Operators need a privacy-aware modular client that survives invalid data, slow networks, offline states, and deployment changes. Planned artifact: Deliver Resilient Public Data Client Capstone with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
Use fetch with explicit method, headers, body, status checks, parsing, abort, and failure-state behavior.
Parse, validate, transform, and serialize JSON while accounting for unsupported values and hostile object shape.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Release Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. content.title: Evidence defense
